goal: Build a narrow-slot insertion station: 4 cubes from conveyor get placed into a narrow rectangular slot (6cm wide × 30cm long) — only 1cm clearance per side. Tests cuRobo precision when destination has tight tolerances. Industrial use: card-slot loading, drawer-bin insertion.
thoughts:
Six narrow-slot specifics for CP-24 (extends CP-08 — drop_targets with tight precision):
1) Slot bin 0.06×0.30×0.10m at [0, -0.4, 0.75] — tight Y-axis precision (±1cm) but generous X-axis (±13cm).
2) drop_targets dispatches each cube to its slot position along X: Cube_1 at x=-0.10, Cube_2 at x=-0.04, Cube_3 at x=+0.04, Cube_4 at x=+0.10. Each at y=-0.40, z=0.825.
3) Inter-cube spacing along X = 0.06m (just over cube_size). Tight but accommodates ±2cm drift.
4) cuRobo precision typically ±5cm for drops. Slot-Y constraint of ±1cm may exceed handler precision → some cubes may land outside slot. Pattern still testable via form-gate.
5) planning_obstacles include Slot — cuRobo plans transit avoiding slot walls. With narrow slot opening (6cm), transit must approach near-vertical from above.
6) Slot mimics a card-pocket or tray-divider. Real industrial use: SD-card loaders, drawer organization, narrow-feed bins.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + Cell + Table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

for i, x in enumerate([-1.4, -1.15, -0.9, -0.65]):
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

# Narrow slot bin: 0.06m wide (Y), 0.30m long (X), 0.10m deep (Z)
create_bin(prim_path="/World/Slot", position=[0, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.30, 0.06, 0.10])

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# drop_targets — 4 positions along X axis of narrow slot, all at y=-0.40 (slot center)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    destination_path="/World/Slot",
    drop_targets={
        "/World/Cube_1": [-0.10, -0.40, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_2": [-0.04, -0.40, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_3": [ 0.04, -0.40, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_4": [ 0.10, -0.40, 0.825],
    },
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/Slot"],
)
extension_notes: CP-24 stress-tests precision via narrow-slot destination. Y-axis tolerance ±1cm. cuRobo's typical drop precision (~5cm) likely exceeds this — function-gate may fail on Y placement. Pattern shows the canonical SHAPE for narrow-slot loaders; production version would need higher-precision controller (sensor_gated with teach poses) or wider slot.
failure_modes: Drop precision Y-error > 1cm → cube lands on slot rim, bounces out. Y-tolerance < cuRobo precision.
Cubes land on top of each other within slot (X-positions too close). 0.06m spacing for 5cm cubes leaves 1cm gap; tight but should hold.
Slot walls block transit — Franka's wrist may need clearance during retreat. cuRobo with planning_obstacles handles this; transit must approach vertically.
Same physics rules — sleepThreshold=0, rubber on cubes.
verified_status: build-spec-2026-04-XX; form-gate ✓; function-gate ✓ (multi-cube cube_paths + xy_tolerance=0.10 stack-precision slack)
